name: Faro de Cala Figuera — circular desde Peguera por Calvià
region_intro: Mountain biking around Calvià, located on Mallorca's southwest coast, offers diverse landscapes for riders. The region features a blend of mountains, including the Serra de Tramuntana and Serra de Na Burguesa, and a picturesque coastline. Terrain varies from flat paths to consistent gradients and technical sections, with extensive Mediterranean pine forests. This varied topography provides a range of mountain bike routes. Best mountain bike trails around Calvià The most popular mountain bike trail is Faro de Cala Figuera — circular desde Peguera por Calvià, a 39.6 miles (63.8 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 33 minutes to complete. This challenging coastal route offers varied terrain and sea views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Son Claret Castle – Santa Ponsa Windmill loop from Peguera, a moderate 23.6 miles (37.9 km) path. This route winds through Mediterranean pine forests and offers scenic viewpoints. Local mountain bikers also love the Portixol Marina – Can Pastilla Beach loop from La Romana (Peguera), a 43.6 miles (70.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas and marinas, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Mountain biking around Calvià is defined by the dramatic peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana, the varied terrain of Finca Pública Galatzó, and scenic coastal routes. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Calvià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Calvià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Calvià?
A: Calvià offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available for exploration. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy paths to more challenging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Calvià?
A: The terrain in Calvià is highly diverse, ranging from flat paths and consistent gradients to technical sections with jumps, berms, and steep descents. You'll encounter rocky single tracks, winding trails through olive groves, and extensive forests of oak and Mediterranean pine, especially in areas like the Serra de Tramuntana and Finca Pública Galatzó.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Calvià?
A: Yes, Calvià has trails suitable for beginners. Approximately 20 of the available routes are classified as easy, offering a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the region. These trails often feature smoother paths and less elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders in Calvià?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Calvià offers numerous demanding routes. About 37 trails are rated as difficult, featuring technical sections, significant elevation changes, and rugged terrain. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009703701/faro-de-cala-figuera-circular-desde-peguera-por-calvia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Cala Figuera — circular desde Peguera por Calvià&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.6-mile route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Calvià?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Calvià are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009703701/faro-de-cala-figuera-circular-desde-peguera-por-calvia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Cala Figuera — circular desde Peguera por Calvià&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31776149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot d'es Moro Viewpoint – Cala Figuera Lighthouse loop from Peguera&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic loops.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Calvià's trails offer access to stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana, the diverse landscapes of Finca Pública Galatzó with its ancient structures, and scenic coastal routes with sea views. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/140523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Galatzó Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/338149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera from Puig de les Basses&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts beautiful beaches and coves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/83139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caló d'en Monjo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/663862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Portals Vells&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves I can explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Calvià offers opportunities to explore caves. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/87423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Coves de Mare de Déu (Portals Vells — Caves of the Virgin Mary)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gènova Caves&lt;/a&gt;. These provide a unique adventure off the bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Calvià?
A: Mallorca's mild climate makes Calvià an ideal destination for mountain biking for most of the year. The warm weather allows for comfortable riding conditions outside of the peak summer heat, making spring, autumn, and even winter excellent times to visit.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Calvià?
A: The mountain bike trails in Calvià are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic peaks of the Serra de Tramuntana to the stunning coastal views, and the variety of trails suitable for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Calvià?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted in detail, the presence of easy trails and multi-use paths like the Paseo de Calvià suggests options for families. The Paseo de Calvià, a 32-kilometer path, connects various population centers and natural spots, offering an accessible option for cycling with children.

Q: Can I use public transport to access mountain bike trails in Calvià?
A: Calvià's infrastructure, including the extensive Paseo de Calvià, connects various population centers, which often have public transport links. While specific public transport details for trailheads are not provided, accessing the general area by bus is feasible, and some routes may be reachable from these points.

Q: Are there any routes that combine mountain biking with coastal views?
A: Yes, Calvià is known for its scenic coastal routes that blend mountain landscapes with stunning sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38263440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portixol Marina – Can Pastilla Beach loop from La Romana (Peguera)&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that leads through coastal areas and marinas, offering beautiful vistas.
tips:
Remains of the old defensive tower located in the municipality of Calviá built between 1579 and 1582.

The tower has a structure with a circular plan and truncated cone elevation. Originally, it was accessed through an external wooden staircase inserted into the wall.
The descent to Cala Figuera requires a lot of surefootedness. The tour as easy to describe is definitely wrong.
Ruins of an old castle located in the Sierra de na Burguesa. A few meters away, just above the building, there is a magnificent viewpoint of the south coast of the island.
